summary: Iteration 18 — Customer Experience metrics + Synthetic User journey end-to-end validation. Backend pytest 28/28 PASSED in 10.5s covering: (a) GET /api/cx/metrics shape — window_seconds (default 300, custom 60), samples count, series list each with [t, page_load_ms, add_to_cart_ms, checkout_ms, error_shown_rate, conversion, perceived_speed], current snapshot, rolling_30s with all 6 keys, annotations list with t_relative (number) + cx_delta dict containing page_load_ms_delta/perceived_speed_delta/error_rate_delta/samples_before/samples_after on every entry; (b) boundary validation — window_seconds=10 → 400, window_seconds=5000 → 400; (c) POST /api/cx/synthetic-user/run shape — started_at, total_ms, total_steps≥3 (observed 4), successful_steps, errors_seen, avg_latency_ms, avg_perceived_speed bounded [0,100], verdict ∈ {delightful, acceptable, frustrating, broken}, verdict_color hex, sri_at_run, steps each with [name, method, path, status_code, latency_ms, perceived_speed, success, error, body_preview]; (d) recent_journeys cap — after 2 runs len(rj)≥2 AND ≤5; (e) perceived_speed scoring — fast steps (<200ms) score 100, all steps bounded [0,100], linear interpolation verified within tolerance 1.0 of formula 100*(1-(lat-200)/1800) for lat∈[200,2000]; (f) in-process total_ms<3000ms (observed ~30-100ms via httpx ASGITransport); (g) Checkout preview anon step returns 401, success=false, errors_seen≥1; (h) regression — /api/healing/topology/schema, /api/healing/fea?granularity=service|component, /api/healing/active-propagations, /api/metrics/correlation, /api/healing/resilience-debt, /api/healing/trend, /api/products, /api/healing/auto-dampen-wave, /api/healing/optimize-sequence, /api/healing/execute-sequence, /api/admin/webhooks/status (admin), /api/auth/login all 200. Frontend Playwright validates: admin login → System Health tab → cx-panel renders between active-propagations-panel (top=967) and conv-correlation-chart (top=2554), with cx top=1990 confirming exact ordering; all 5 score tiles (cx-score-page-load=12ms, cx-score-cart=5ms, cx-score-errors=6.08%, cx-score-conversion=1.00%, cx-score-perceived=100/100) present; placeholder text 'Click Run Synthetic User to see a real customer's journey' visible before any run; clicking cx-run-synthetic-btn POSTs /api/cx/synthetic-user/run, cx-journey-card appears with verdict pill ('Acceptable' yellow), '1 error shown' badge, and exactly 4 cx-journey-step-N entries showing method (GET), path (/api/products, /api/products/{id}, /api/products?category=Bakery, /api/orders), status (200/200/200/401), latency (3ms/2ms/2ms/30ms), and UX score; cx-chart renders with dual Y-axes (perceived_speed left 0-100 green, page_load right orange) and ⚡ ReferenceLine markers for healing annotations (e.g. '-25s rate_limit @ API'); cx-healing-impacts list visible with per-annotation Page+10ms/UX+0.0/Err+6.00% deltas. No console errors blocking; only benign recharts width=-1 warnings during initial paint of off-screen panels.

design_issues.screen: Dashboard / System Health / Customer Experience Panel
issues: Page Load tile shows '12 ms' rolling-30s avg derived from API-node latency proxy — but Add to Cart tile sublabel says 'cache-latency proxy' (5 ms). Operators may not realise these are derived from the same physical sample (page_load * 0.4) rather than independently measured. Suggest a tooltip or 'derived' badge.

cx-chart Y-axis (right/page_load orange) auto-scales to data peak (~14ms in test) which is fine, but with the Perceived Speed line locked to 100/100 the chart visually flatlines for the green series. Consider showing perceived_speed only when it dips below 100 OR adding a band/gradient to make 'all-green' periods feel intentional rather than 'broken chart'.

Healing impact row '−25s rate_limit @ API · Page +10ms · UX +0.0 · Err +6.00%' uses '+' for both improvements and regressions; the Err badge is colored red (correct, regression) but the +10ms Page is also red while UX +0.0 is green — minor ambiguity. Consider neutral colour for delta=0 and explicit ↑/↓ icons for clarity.

Run Synthetic User button + step animation re-enables after each click but the existing journey card stays mounted with old step opacities until 500ms*N animation completes — looks staggered. Consider clearing journey state synchronously on click for an instant 'reset' feel.

action_items: (Cosmetic) Tooltip on Add to Cart tile clarifying it is derived from page_load_ms × 0.4 (cache-latency proxy) — currently the metric looks like an independent measurement.
(Cosmetic) Neutral colour + directional icon for delta=0 in cx-healing-impacts row so +0.0/0% deltas don't share the red/green palette of true regressions/improvements.

(Code-quality, low-priority) CustomerExperiencePanel.jsx line 48 `catch (e) { /* silent */ }` swallows axios errors on /api/cx/metrics polling — same pattern flagged in Iter 17 ActivePropagationsPanel. Add dev-only console.warn or non-blocking toast after >3 consecutive failures.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now 5207 lines (was 4951 in Iter 17) — splitting into backend/cx/tracker.py (CustomerExperienceTracker), backend/cx/synthetic_user.py (run_synthetic_user_journey + step helper), backend/routes/cx.py for FastAPI routes is overdue.

run_synthetic_user_journey opens a NEW AsyncClient(transport=ASGITransport(app=app)) per step (4 clients per call). That's ~4× constructor overhead per journey. Consider a single AsyncClient context manager wrapping the whole journey — would also let you share connection pool and reduce total_ms by 30-50%.

The 'View product' fallback step (server.py:4700) appends a step dict missing `method`, `path`, `status_code`, `body_preview`, `error` keys when db.products.find_one raises — this would break test_run_synthetic_step_keys if the DB ever errors. Currently green only because Mongo is healthy. Suggest using the same `step()` helper or normalising the fallback entry.

CustomerExperiencePanel.jsx line 173 filter `a.t_relative.toFixed(0)` on ReferenceLine — if t_relative is somehow NaN (e.g. a future schema change), .toFixed crashes the whole panel. The `typeof === 'number'` filter handles undefined/null but NOT NaN. Consider Number.isFinite(a.t_relative).

CustomerExperiencePanel.jsx line 67 destructures j.steps without null-check — if backend returns 500 the catch block fires (good), but if backend returns 200 with a malformed body (no `steps` key) it would throw. Consider validating response shape.

cx_tracker.snapshot() acquires correlation_tracker._lock while holding its own lock (server.py:774-775) — risk of deadlock if any other code path takes correlation_tracker._lock then attempts cx_tracker._lock. Currently safe (no such caller exists), but document the lock ordering or use trylock with timeout.

Synthetic-user steps include `body_preview` which serialises `{key1, key2...}` for dicts — for objects with sensitive keys (e.g. JWT, password reset tokens in a future endpoint) this leaks key names to the journey log. Consider an allowlist of safe path patterns OR truncating to only the first key.
test_credentials: admin@freshcart.com / admin123 (source: /app/memory/test_credentials.md). httpOnly cookie auth verified on /api/admin/webhooks/status.
seed_data_creation: None permanent. Each synthetic-user run inserts an in-memory journey into cx_tracker._journeys (capped at 20 internally, exposed at 5 via snapshot). No DB writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://delivery-metrics-hub-1.preview.emergentagent.com pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration18_cx_metrics.py -v` (~13s; calls /api/cx/synthetic-user/run ~7× across tests). Frontend: login admin → System Health (resilience) tab → assert cx-panel between active-propagations-panel and conv-correlation-chart by getBoundingClientRect().top ordering. Click cx-run-synthetic-btn → cx-journey-card appears within ~2-5s with 4 steps; the 4th step (Checkout preview anon) is EXPECTED to show status_code=401 in red — that is by design per spec note (counts as one error_shown to exercise verdict logic). The cx-chart appears once series.length≥2; first poll ~4s after panel mount, second ~8s — wait at least 8-10s after navigating before asserting cx-chart presence. Annotations populate the cx-healing-impacts list only when correlation_tracker has emitted at least one healing annotation in the past 300s AND that annotation has cx_delta.samples_before>0 AND samples_after>0 (i.e. CX samples bracketing the heal). Iter 13-17 regressions all green.
rca of the issue: N/A — no failures found. Three regression-test corrections during initial run were due to my test paths being wrong (not backend bugs): (1) endpoints live under /api/healing/* not /api/* — spec was abbreviated; (2) /api/healing/auto-dampen-wave is POST and requires source/granularity/fault_strength/steps/critical_arrival_threshold (it's an action endpoint, not a snapshot); (3) /api/healing/optimize-sequence schema requires `stressed_nodes` (not `candidates`) and each entry needs `node` field (not just `target_node`). After corrections all 28 tests pass. The 'Checkout preview' step returning 401 with success=false counted as errors_seen=1 is the documented expected behavior per the agent_to_agent_context_note — this drives verdict='acceptable' (avg_perceived≥60 AND errors_seen≤1) which is what the UI displayed.
